speaker: DUNCAN
text: This castle hath a pleasant seat. The air

speaker: DUNCAN
text: Nimbly and sweetly recommends itself

speaker: DUNCAN
text: Unto our gentle senses.

speaker: BANQUO
text: This guest of summer,

speaker: BANQUO
text: The temple-haunting martlet, does approve,

speaker: BANQUO
text: By his loved mansionry, that the heaven’s breath

speaker: BANQUO
text: Smells wooingly here. No jutty, frieze,

speaker: BANQUO
text: Buttress, nor coign of vantage, but this bird

speaker: BANQUO
text: Hath made his pendant bed and procreant cradle.

speaker: BANQUO
text: Where they most breed and haunt, I have observed,

speaker: BANQUO
text: The air is delicate.

speaker: DUNCAN
text: See, see our honored hostess!—

speaker: DUNCAN
text: The love that follows us sometime is our trouble,

speaker: DUNCAN
text: Which still we thank as love. Herein I teach you

speaker: DUNCAN
text: How you shall bid God ’ild us for your pains

speaker: DUNCAN
text: And thank us for your trouble.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: All our service,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: In every point twice done and then done double,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Were poor and single business to contend

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Against those honors deep and broad wherewith

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Your Majesty loads our house. For those of old,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: And the late dignities heaped up to them,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: We rest your hermits.

speaker: DUNCAN
text: Where’s the Thane of Cawdor?

speaker: DUNCAN
text: We coursed him at the heels and had a purpose

speaker: DUNCAN
text: To be his purveyor; but he rides well,

speaker: DUNCAN
text: And his great love, sharp as his spur, hath helped him

speaker: DUNCAN
text: To his home before us. Fair and noble hostess,

speaker: DUNCAN
text: We are your guest tonight.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Your servants ever

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Have theirs, themselves, and what is theirs in compt

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: To make their audit at your Highness’ pleasure,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACBETH
text: Still to return your own.

speaker: DUNCAN
text: Give me your hand.

speaker: DUNCAN
text: Conduct me to mine host. We love him highly

speaker: DUNCAN
text: And shall continue our graces towards him.

speaker: DUNCAN
text: By your leave, hostess.
